assumptions: GitHub API is accessible for conflict detection via mergeable_state
The existing mergeDefaultBranch() function can be safely modified with backward-compatible options
The merge-resolve agent will have proper git permissions in the CI environment
All existing tests will continue to pass after modifications
review_questions: Is the backward compatibility of mergeDefaultBranch() properly maintained for existing callers?
Does the auto-skip logic for resolve-conflicts stage correctly detect when no conflicts exist?
Is the fix to runFixMode() properly handling the merge conflict case without silently failing?
Should the new 'merge' mode be documented in user-facing documentation?

Ambiguity: 2 points (detailed spec with file paths and line numbers). Dependency: 8 points (internal pipeline systems + GitHub API). Total: 79 - Very Complex+ tier requiring clarify stage.
